justification: LagnaMaster uses swe.MEAN_NODE (traditional Indian astrology). PyJHora defaults to swe.TRUE_NODE (modern software convention). Difference typically 0.3-1.5 degrees. BPHS does not specify mathematical model for nodes. Mean node is the traditional choice.
source: Swiss Ephemeris Manual §2.3; BPHS does not distinguish true vs mean node; traditional practice uses mean

justification: Same as SYS-001. Ketu = Rahu + 180 degrees. Inherits the same true/mean node discrepancy.

LagnaMaster applies topocentric correction (swe.set_topo + SEFLG_TOPOCTR) per Swiss Ephemeris Manual §2.3 — accounts for observer's position on Earth surface. PyJHora uses geocentric Moon (no SEFLG_TOPOCTR). Difference 0.2-0.9 degrees, varies by latitude and Moon altitude, not systematic in one direction (136 LM>PJH, 127 LM<PJH). Topocentric is astronomically more accurate for natal chart calculation.
source: Swiss Ephemeris Manual §2.3; src/ephemeris.py line 217-220 (S161 fix); PyJHora drik.py has no SEFLG_TOPOCTR
